words.definition: a symbol that represents a number: digit
examples: He has a six-figure salary. [=he has a salary of at least $100,000]

words.definition: something (such as a situation) that is made up of two opposite things and that seems impossible but is actually true or possible
examples: It is a paradox that computers need maintenance so often, since they are meant to save people time.

words.definition: of or relating to a person's spirit
examples: Doctors must consider the emotional and spiritual needs of their patients.
I'm working on my spiritual growth/development. [=the growth of my mind and spirit]

words.definition: to cause (someone or something) to be widely known and accepted
examples: establish a claim
The film established her as a star.
She established a reputation as a hard worker.
The word is now established as part of the English language.

words.definition: not usual or normal: strange
examples: It seems peculiar that he would leave town and not tell anybody.
The dog's peculiar behavior worried them.
She got a peculiar feeling when the phone rang.
She had a peculiar expression on her face.

words.definition: to grow or develop successfully: to flourish or succeed
examples: Business is thriving. [=booming]
The region thrived [=prospered] under his rule.
plants that thrive [=grow well] in the desert

words.definition: feeling or showing doubt about doing something: not willing or eager to do something
words.arabicTranslation: غير راغب في فعل شيء، لكن قد يفعله إذا كان ضروريًا
examples: We were reluctant to get involved.
He might agree but seems reluctant to admit it.
a reluctant participant

words.definition: actual, real, or true: not false or fake
collocations: often used in the phrase the genuine article to refer to someone or something that is not a copy or substitute
examples: genuine leather
The signature is genuine. [=authentic]
There has been a genuine improvement in the economy in recent months.
Don't fall for cheap imitations
this is the genuine article.

words.definition: to make (someone) believe something that is not true
examples: Her parents punished her for trying to deceive them.
The wall doesn't look real—it wouldn't deceive [=fool] a child.
He was accused of deceiving the customer about the condition of the car.
People who think they can eat whatever they want without harming their health are deceiving themselves.

words.definition: to change the natural, normal, or original shape, appearance, or sound of (something) in a way that is usually not attractive or pleasing
words.arabicTranslation: يحرّف أو يشوّه الحقيقة قليلاً عن شكلها الأصلي
examples: Her face was distorted by pain.
The odd camera angle distorted her figure in the photograph.
The sound of the guitar was distorted.
Heat caused the plastic to distort.

words.definition: not well-known: not known to most people
examples: obscure books/titles
a little antique shop in an obscure [=out-of-the-way] corner of the city

words.definition: to not be harmed or affected by (something)
examples: cookware that can withstand high temperatures
I couldn't withstand the rigors of army life.

words.definition: to become worse or to make (something) worse or more severe
words.arabicTranslation: يتصاعد، خصوصًا مع conflict / problem / situation
examples: The conflict has escalated into an all-out war.
a time of escalating tensions/violence
We are trying not to escalate the violence.

words.definition: the power to act that voters give to their elected leaders
examples: He won the election so convincingly that he believed he had been given a mandate for change/reform.
He believed he had been given a mandate (from the people) to implement his policies.
There is no mandate to raise taxes.

words.definition: to give power or permission to (someone or something)
words.arabicTranslation: يعطي إذنًا رسميًا أو صلاحية لفعل شيء، خصوصًا في سياق قانوني أو مهني.
examples: Only authorized personnel [=people who have been given special permission] can enter this area.
I authorized him to use my name.
She was authorized [=empowered] to act for her husband.

words.definition: able to be seen: clearly shown or visible
words.arabicTranslation: يظهر كعلامة أو نتيجة، وغالبًا كلمة رسمية أكثر
examples: Their sadness was manifest in their faces.
His love for literature is manifest in his large library.
There was manifest confusion in the streets.

words.definition: to avoid dealing with or facing (something)
words.arabicTranslation: يتجنب الإجابة المباشرة أو يتهرّب من التعامل مع موقف صعب.
examples: a politician skilled at evading difficult questions
The governor has been accused of evading the issue.

words.definition: to avoid being stopped by (something, such as a law or rule): to get around (something) in a clever and sometimes dishonest way
examples: We circumvented the problem by using a different program.
He found a way to circumvent the law.

words.definition: to hit something and suddenly change direction
examples: The ball deflected off the goalie's shoulder.

words.definition: to make (someone) less determined, hopeful, or confident
words.arabicTranslation: يجعل الشخص يفقد الثقة أو الحماس لفعل شيء كان ينوي فعله.
examples: Try not to let losing discourage you.
We were discouraged by their lack of enthusiasm.
Students may get discouraged by activities that are too advanced.

words.definition: to convince (someone) not to do something
words.arabicTranslation: يقنع شخصًا ألا يفعل شيئًا بالحوار أو النصيحة
examples: Our warnings did not dissuade them.
He tried to dissuade them from going.

words.definition: to officially end or stop (something, such as a law): to completely do away with (something)
words.arabicTranslation: يلغي نظامًا أو قانونًا أو ممارسة قديمة بالكامل
examples: abolish slavery/apartheid
He is in favor of abolishing the death penalty.

words.definition: to make (someone or something) weaker or less effective usually in a secret or gradual way
examples: She tried to undermine my authority by complaining about me to my boss.
The events of the past year have undermined people's confidence in the government.

words.definition: to think of (someone or something) as being lower in ability, influence, or value than that person or thing actually is
words.arabicTranslation: يظن أن الشخص أقل قدرة مما هو عليه
examples: Never underestimate the importance of a good education.
Her talent has always been underestimated.

words.definition: to place too low a value on (something)
words.arabicTranslation: يقلّل من قيمة الشخص أو لا يعطيه ما يستحقه
examples: Experts undervalued the stock.

words.definition: a place where drinks and entertainment are provided for soldiers at a military base or camp
words.arabicTranslation: كافتيريا (في مدرسة، جامعة، شركة، أو معسكر)

words.definition: a painter who practices impressionism
words.arabicTranslation: انطباعي (فنان أو رسام ينتمي إلى المدرسة الانطباعية)

words.definition: enough for some need or requirement
examples: Be sure to allow adequate [=sufficient, enough] time for the paint to dry.
The garden hasn't been getting adequate water.
The food was more than adequate for the six of us.

words.definition: to prepare and use (soil) for growing plants
examples: Prehistoric peoples settled the area and began to cultivate the land.
Some of the fields are cultivated while others lie fallow.

words.definition: to help someone or something pay for the costs of (something)
examples: The state subsidizes housing for low-income families.
She feels that private businesses should not be subsidized by taxpayers.
The company subsidizes health insurance for its employees.
subsidized agriculture/housing

words.definition: a way of earning money in order to live
examples: Many fishermen believe that the new regulations threaten their livelihoods.
(formal) He claims he lost a source/means of livelihood when he was injured.

words.definition: very fat: fat in a way that is unhealthy
examples: providing medical treatment for obese patients

words.definition: the hair that grows on a man's cheeks and chin
examples: He grew a beard and mustache.

words.definition: a person whose job is to cut men's hair
examples: He goes to a barber downtown.
He went to the barber's [=(chiefly US) barbershop] to get a haircut.

words.definition: not clear in meaning: stated in a way that is general and not specific
examples: The instructions she left were vague and difficult to follow.
He gave only a vague answer.
The judges determined that the law was too vague to be fairly enforced.

words.definition: the act of using a word, phrase, etc., that repeats something else and is therefore unnecessary
examples: Avoid redundancy in your writing.

words.definition: having a high temperature
examples: hot August nights
hot and humid weather
taking a hot bath/shower
a hot climate/country

words.definition: a covering for the head that often has a brim and a rounded or flat top
examples: a straw hat

words.definition: a vehicle that has wings and an engine and can carry people or things in the air: airplane
examples: a cargo plane
We will be traveling to Rome by plane.
a plane crash/ride

words.definition: not having curves, bends, or angles
examples: a straight line/edge
She has long, straight hair.
Keep your back straight.

words.definition: not in or at any place
examples: I have nowhere to go/sit/live.
These facts are nowhere stated (in the book).
Nowhere is there more of a population problem than in this city.
The book is nowhere to be found. = The book is nowhere to be seen. = The book is nowhere in sight.

words.definition: a place where people go for vacations
examples: a ski/beach/golf resort
a resort hotel

words.definition: a line of people who are waiting for something
examples: The people formed a queue [=(US) line] at the ticket window.
We were forced to stand/wait in a queue.
a bus/taxi queue

words.definition: a small vehicle that is used for traveling on water
examples: a fishing boat
He tied the boat to the dock.

words.definition: easily broken or damaged
examples: the flower's fragile petals
Her health has always been very fragile.
fragile bones
an artist with a fragile ego

words.definition: more than usually: very extremely
words.arabicTranslation:
خصوصًا
I enjoy reading, especially history books.
examples: He was especially concerned about the contract.
an especially good essay
There is nothing especially radical about that idea.
Winning this tournament was especially satisfying.

words.definition: a gray bird that is common in cities and that has a fat body and short legs see also clay pigeon homing pigeon stool pigeon

words.arabicTranslation: عُرضة لـ / ضعيف أمام / مُعرّض للخطر

words.definition: to think of or create (something that is not real) in your mind
examples: a writer who has imagined an entire world of amazing creatures

words.definition: a hard flat surface for vehicles, people, and animals to travel on
examples: a paved/gravel/dirt road
We'll cross the road up ahead at the crosswalk.
He drove off the road.
We parked by the side of the road.

words.definition: not dead: having life
examples: I was taught to respect all living things.
living beings/creatures/animals/plants
His aunt is his closest living relative.
He's one of our greatest living authors.

words.definition: the act of choosing: the act of picking or deciding between two or more possibilities
examples: He knew he had to make a choice. [=choose one thing or another]
He has some important choices to make.
You made a good/bad choice.
She was faced with a difficult choice.

words.definition: a very young child
examples: She had the baby yesterday. = She gave birth to the baby yesterday.
Are you expecting a baby? [=are you pregnant?]
I hear the baby crying.
The baby is just learning to crawl.

words.definition: the state of being a mother or a father
examples: the joys of parenthood

words.definition: the quality or fact of staying the same at different times
examples: His statements on this subject have lacked consistency.

words.definition: allowing you to do something easily or without trouble
examples: When is a convenient time for you to meet?
The controls are located in a convenient spot on the dashboard.
It might be more convenient to use a calculator, rather than adding the numbers yourself.
a convenient method/way/means of cleaning windows

words.definition: the act of following and trying to catch a person, animal, etc.
examples: Police caught the bank robbers after a high-speed chase on the highway.
a car chase

words.definition: to have or experience (problems, difficulties, etc.)
examples: We encountered problems early in the project.
The pilot told us that we might encounter turbulence during the flight.
Her suggestion has encountered a lot of opposition.

words.definition: the act of doing something that is not allowed by a law or rule
examples: a serious violation of the law
A second violation was called on the basketball player.
a moving violation = a traffic violation [=the act of breaking a law while driving]
He was arrested for violation of his parole.

words.definition: to publicly or officially say that you support or approve of (someone or something)
examples: The newspaper has endorsed the conservative candidate for mayor.
The committee must endorse [=approve] the decision.
We do not endorse their position.

words.definition: able to cause people to do or believe something: able to persuade people
examples: We weren't shown any persuasive evidence that he had committed the crime.
a very persuasive [=convincing] argument
a persuasive salesman

words.definition: a small town in the country
examples: fishing/mining villages [=villages in which most people fish/mine as a job]

words.definition: an area of low land between hills or mountains
examples: the Shenandoah (River) Valley

words.definition: impossible to understand: not comprehensible
examples: I found his behavior utterly incomprehensible.
It's incomprehensible to me that he could have acted that way.
an incomprehensible theory/decision

words.definition: able to be understood
examples: a comprehensible [=intelligible] explanation
They spoke in barely comprehensible slang. [=slang that was extremely difficult to understand]

words.definition: causing a feeling that something is wrong or that someone is behaving wrongly: causing suspicion
examples: We were instructed to report any suspicious activity/behavior in the neighborhood.
The suspicious vehicle was reported to police.
He died under suspicious circumstances. [=under circumstances that suggest a crime may have been committed]
Suspicious characters were seen hanging around the bank.
